examples.title: FCFS Disk Scheduling
examples.description: Implementation of First-Come-First-Served disk scheduling algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FCFS_Scheduler(requests[], start_position)
BEGIN
    current_position ← start_position
    total_seek_time ← 0
    
    PRINT "FCFS Disk Scheduling"
    PRINT "Start Position:", start_position
    
    FOR each request IN requests DO
        seek_distance ← ABS(request - current_position)
        total_seek_time ← total_seek_time + seek_distance
        current_position ← request
        PRINT "Serviced track:", request, "Seek:", seek_distance
    END FOR
    
    PRINT "Total Seek Time:", total_seek_time
    PRINT "Average Seek Time:", total_seek_time / LENGTH(requests)
END
examples.explanation: FCFS processes requests in the order they arrived. It calculates the absolute difference between current head position and requested track to compute seek time. Simple but may result in excessive head movement.

examples.title: SSTF Disk Scheduling
examples.description: Implementation of Shortest Seek Time First disk scheduling algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SSTF_Scheduler(requests[], start_position)
BEGIN
    current_position ← start_position
    total_seek_time ← 0
    remaining ← COPY(requests)
    visited ← EMPTY_SET
    
    PRINT "SSTF Disk Scheduling"
    PRINT "Start Position:", start_position
    
    WHILE remaining is not empty DO
        min_distance ← INFINITY
        closest_request ← -1
        
        FOR each request IN remaining DO
            distance ← ABS(request - current_position)
            IF distance < min_distance THEN
                min_distance ← distance
                closest_request ← request
            END IF
        END FOR
        
        total_seek_time ← total_seek_time + min_distance
        current_position ← closest_request
        REMOVE closest_request FROM remaining
        PRINT "Serviced track:", closest_request, "Seek:", min_distance
    END WHILE
    
    PRINT "Total Seek Time:", total_seek_time
    PRINT "Average Seek Time:", total_seek_time / LENGTH(requests)
END
examples.explanation: SSTF selects the request closest to the current head position at each step, minimizing seek time between consecutive operations. Similar to SJF CPU scheduling. May cause starvation for distant requests.

examples.title: SCAN Disk Scheduling
examples.description: Python implementation of SCAN (elevator) disk scheduling algorithm
examples.code:
def scan_scheduler(requests, start_position, disk_size=200, direction="up"):
    """
    SCAN Disk Scheduling Algorithm
    Args:
        requests: list of track numbers to service
        start_position: initial head position
        disk_size: maximum track number
        direction: "up" or "down"
    """
    total_seek_time = 0
    current_position = start_position
    
    # Sort requests
    sorted_requests = sorted(requests)
    
    # Split into two lists: less than and greater than start
    left = [r for r in sorted_requests if r < start_position]
    right = [r for r in sorted_requests if r >= start_position]
    
    # Sort both lists
    left.sort(reverse=True)  # reverse for descending order
    right.sort()
    
    print(f"SCAN Disk Scheduling (direction: {direction})")
    print(f"Start Position: {start_position}")
    
    if direction == "up":
        # Go up first
        for request in right:
            seek = abs(request - current_position)
            total_seek_time += seek
            current_position = request
            print(f"Serviced: {request}, Seek: {seek}")
        
        # Go to end
        seek = abs(disk_size - 1 - current_position)
        total_seek_time += seek
        current_position = disk_size - 1
        
        # Go down
        for request in left:
            seek = abs(request - current_position)
            total_seek_time += seek
            current_position = request
            print(f"Serviced: {request}, Seek: {seek}")
    
    print(f"Total Seek Time: {total_seek_time}")
    print(f"Average Seek Time: {total_seek_time / len(requests):.2f}")
    
    return total_seek_time

# Example usage
requests = [98, 183, 41, 122, 14, 124, 65, 67]
scan_scheduler(requests, 53)
examples.explanation: SCAN algorithm moves the head in one direction servicing all requests, then reverses and services remaining requests. Called elevator algorithm because it behaves like an elevator going up then down. Provides good throughput and relatively uniform wait times.

examples.title: Calculate Average Disk Access Time
examples.description: Program to calculate and display average disk access time components
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_Disk_Access_Time
BEGIN
    INPUT seek_time          // Average seek time in ms
    INPUT rpm                // Rotations per minute
    INPUT transfer_rate      // Transfer rate in MB/s
    INPUT sector_size        // Size to read in bytes
    
    // Calculate rotational delay
    rotation_time ← 60 / rpm                    // Time for one rotation in seconds
    avg_rotational_delay ← rotation_time / 2    // Average is half a rotation
    avg_rotational_delay_ms ← avg_rotational_delay * 1000
    
    // Calculate transfer time
    transfer_rate_bps ← transfer_rate * 1024 * 1024  // Convert to bytes/sec
    transfer_time_sec ← sector_size / transfer_rate_bps
    transfer_time_ms ← transfer_time_sec * 1000
    
    // Calculate total average access time
    total_access_time ← seek_time + avg_rotational_delay_ms + transfer_time_ms
    
    PRINT "DISK ACCESS TIME ANALYSIS"
    PRINT "==========================="
    PRINT "Average Seek Time:", seek_time, "ms"
    PRINT "Average Rotational Delay:", avg_rotational_delay_ms, "ms"
    PRINT "Transfer Time:", transfer_time_ms, "ms"
    PRINT "---------------------------"
    PRINT "TOTAL Average Access Time:", total_access_time, "ms"
    
    // Calculate percentage contribution
    PRINT ""
    PRINT "Contribution Breakdown:"
    PRINT "Seek Time:" (seek_time/total_access_time)*100 "%"
    PRINT "Rotational Delay:" (avg_rotational_delay_ms/total_access_time)*100 "%"
    PRINT "Transfer Time:" (transfer_time_ms/total_access_time)*100 "%"
END

// Example calculation with disk parameters
// seek_time = 5ms, rpm = 7200, transfer_rate = 80 MB/s, sector_size = 4096
// Rotation time = 60/7200 = 0.00833s = 8.33ms
// Avg rotational delay = 8.33/2 = 4.17ms
// Transfer time = 4096/(80*1024*1024) = 0.000049s = 0.049ms
// Total = 5 + 4.17 + 0.049 = 9.22ms
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates the three main components of disk access time: seek time (arm movement), rotational delay (waiting for sector), and transfer time (actual data movement). It then sums them to get total average access time.
